94.text: And when the caravan departed [from Egypt], their father said, "Indeed, I find the smell of Joseph [and would say that he was alive] if you did not think me weakened in mind."

94.author: Sarwar
94.text: When the caravan left the town, their father said, "I smell Joseph's scent. I hope that you will not accuse me of senility".

94.author: Shakir
94.text: And when the caravan had departed, their father said: Most surely I perceive the greatness of Yusuf, unless you pronounce me to be weak in judgment.

94.author: Wahiduddin Khan
94.text: When the caravan set out from Egypt, their father [in Canaan] said, "You may think I am senile, but I certainly perceive the breath of Joseph."

94.author: Yusuf Ali
94.text: When the caravan left (Egypt), their father said: "I do indeed scent the presence of Joseph: Nay, think me not a dotard."

94.author: Transliteration
94.text: Walamma fasalati alAAeeru qala aboohum innee laajidu reeha yoosufa lawla an tufannidooni

95.author: Ahmed Ali
95.text: They said: "By God, you are still persisting in your old delusion."

95.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
95.text: They said, “By Allah, you are still deeply engrossed in the same old love of yours.”

95.author: Arberry
95.text: They said, 'By God, thou art certainly in thy ancient error.

95.author: Daryabadi
95.text: They said: by Allah! verily thou art in thine old-time error.

95.author: Hilali & Khan
95.text: They said: "By Allah! Certainly, you are in your old error."

95.author: Itani
95.text: They said, “By God, you are still in your old confusion.”

95.author: Maududi
95.text: They said: "Surely you are still in your same old craze."

95.author: Mubarakpuri
95.text: They said: "By Allah! Certainly, you are in your old Dalal (error)."

95.author: Pickthall
95.text: (Those around him) said: By Allah, lo! thou art in thine old aberration.

95.author: Qarai
95.text: They said, ‘By God, you persist in your inveterate error.’

95.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
95.text: 'By Allah' they said, 'this is but your old illusion'

95.author: Saheeh International
95.text: They said, "By Allah, indeed you are in your [same] old error."

95.author: Sarwar
95.text: His people said, "By God, you are still making the same old error".

95.author: Shakir
95.text: They said: By Allah, you are most surely in your old error.

95.author: Wahiduddin Khan
95.text: They said, "By God, you still persist in your illusions!"

95.author: Yusuf Ali
95.text: They said: "By Allah! truly thou art in thine old wandering mind."

95.author: Transliteration
95.text: Qaloo taAllahi innaka lafee dalalika alqadeemi

96.author: Ahmed Ali
96.text: Then, as the harbinger of happy news arrived and put the garment over his face his eyesight was restored. He said: "Did I not tell you? I know from God what you do not know."

96.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
96.text: Then when the bearer of glad tidings came, he laid the shirt on his face, he therefore immediately regained his eyesight*; he said, “Was I not telling you? I know the great traits of Allah which you do not know!” (This was a miracle that took place by applying Prophet Yusuf’s shirt.)

96.author: Arberry
96.text: But when the bearer of good tidings came to him, and laid it on his face, forthwith he saw once again. He said, 'Did I not tell you I know from God that you know not?'

96.author: Daryabadi
96.text: Then, when the bringer of the glad tidings came, he cast it upon his face and he became clear-sighted. He said: said I not unto you, verily I know from Allah that which ye know not.

96.author: Hilali & Khan
96.text: Then, when the bearer of the glad tidings arrived, he cast it (the shirt) over his face, and he became clear-sighted. He said: "Did I not say to you, 'I know from Allah that which you know not. 

96.author: Itani
96.text: Then, when the bearer of good news arrived, he laid it over his face, and he regained his sight. He said, “Did I not say to you that I know from God what you do not know?”

96.author: Maududi
96.text: And when the bearer of good news came he threw Joseph's shirt over Jacob's face, whereupon he regained his sight, and said: "Did I not tell you that I know from Allah what you do not know?"

96.author: Mubarakpuri
96.text: Then, when the bearer of the good news arrived, he cast it (the shirt) over his face, and his vision returned. He said: "Did I not say to you, `I know from Allah that which you know not"'

96.author: Pickthall
96.text: Then, when the bearer of glad tidings came, he laid it on his face and he became a seer once more. He said: Said I not unto you that I know from Allah that which ye know not?

96.author: Qarai
96.text: When the bearer of good news arrived, he cast it on his face, and he regained his sight. He said, ‘Did I not tell you, ‘‘I know from Allah what you do not know?’’ ’

96.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
96.text: And when the bearer of glad tidings arrived, he laid Joseph's shirt over him, and he saw once again. He said: 'Did I not tell you that I know from Allah what you do not know'

96.author: Saheeh International
96.text: And when the bearer of good tidings arrived, he cast it over his face, and he returned [once again] seeing. He said, "Did I not tell you that I know from Allah that which you do not know?"

96.author: Sarwar
96.text: When someone brought him the glad news, Joseph's shirt was placed on his face and his eye-sight was restored, he said, "Did I not tell you that I know about God that which you do not know?"

96.author: Shakir
96.text: So when the bearer of good news came he cast it on his face, so forthwith he regained his sight. He said: Did I not say to you that I know from Allah what you do not know?

96.author: Wahiduddin Khan
96.text: But when the bearer of the good news arrived and cast the shirt on Jacob's face, his eyesight returned and he said, "Did I not tell you that I know from God what you do not know?"

96.author: Yusuf Ali
96.text: Then when the bearer of the good news came, He cast (the shirt) over his face, and he forthwith regained clear sight. He said: "Did I not say to you, 'I know from Allah that which ye know not?'"

96.author: Transliteration
96.text: Falamma an jaa albasheeru alqahu AAala wajhihi fairtadda baseeran qala alam aqul lakum innee aAAlamu mina Allahi ma la taAAlamoona

97.author: Ahmed Ali
97.text: Said (the sons): "O our father, pray for us that our sins be forgiven, for we are really sinners."

97.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
97.text: They said, “O our father! Seek forgiveness for our sins, for we were indeed guilty.”

97.author: Arberry
97.text: They said, 'Our father, ask forgiveness of our crimes for us; for certainly we have been sinful.'

97.author: Daryabadi
97.text: They said: our father! pray for forgiveness of our sins for us; verily we have been sinners.

97.author: Hilali & Khan
97.text: They said: "O our father! Ask forgiveness (from Allah) for our sins, indeed we have been sinners."

97.author: Itani
97.text: They said, “Father, pray for the forgiveness of our sins; we were indeed at fault.”

97.author: Maududi
97.text: They said: "Father! Pray for the forgiveness of our sins; we were truly guilty."

97.author: Mubarakpuri
97.text: They said: "O our father! Ask forgiveness (from Allah) for our sins, indeed we have been sinners."

97.author: Pickthall
97.text: They said: O our father! Ask forgiveness of our sins for us, for lo! we were sinful.

97.author: Qarai
97.text: They said, ‘Father! Plead [with Allah] for forgiveness of our sins! We have indeed been erring.’

97.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
97.text: His sons said: 'Father, ask forgiveness for our sins. We have indeed been sinners'

97.author: Saheeh International
97.text: They said, "O our father, ask for us forgiveness of our sins; indeed, we have been sinners."

97.author: Sarwar
97.text: They said, "Father, ask God to forgive our sins; we have certainly sinned".

97.author: Shakir
97.text: They said: O our father! ask forgiveness of our faults for us, surely we were sinners.

97.author: Wahiduddin Khan
97.text: They said, "O our father! Ask forgiveness for our sins -- we were truly in the wrong."

97.author: Yusuf Ali
97.text: They said: "O our father! ask for us forgiveness for our sins, for we were truly at fault."

97.author: Transliteration
97.text: Qaloo ya abana istaghfir lana thunoobana inna kunna khatieena

98.author: Ahmed Ali
98.text: "I will ask my Lord to forgive you," he replied, "for He is forgiving and kind."

98.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
98.text: He said, “I shall soon seek forgiveness for you from my Lord; indeed He only is the Oft Forgiving, the Most Merciful.”

98.author: Arberry
98.text: He said, 'Assuredly I will ask my Lord to forgive you; He is the All-forgiving, the All-compassionate.'

98.author: Daryabadi
98.text: He said: presently I shall pray for forgiveness for you of my Lord; verily He! only He is the Forgiving, the Merciful.

98.author: Hilali & Khan
98.text: He said: "I will ask my Lord for forgiveness for you, verily He! Only He is the Oft-Forgiving, the Most Merciful."

98.author: Itani
98.text: He said, “I will ask my Lord to forgive you. He is the Forgiver, the Most Merciful.”

98.author: Maududi
98.text: He said: "I shall pray to my Lord for your forgiveness, for He, and indeed He alone, is Ever Forgiving, Most Merciful."

98.author: Mubarakpuri
98.text: He said: "I will ask my Lord for forgiveness for you, verily, He! Only He is the Oft-Forgiving, the Most Merciful."

98.author: Pickthall
98.text: He said: I shall ask forgiveness for you of my Lord. Lo! He is the Forgiving, the Merciful.

98.author: Qarai
98.text: He said, ‘I shall plead with my Lord to forgive you; indeed He is the All-forgiving, the All-merciful.’

98.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
98.text: He said: 'I shall ask my Lord to forgive you. He is Forgiving, the Most Merciful'

98.author: Saheeh International
98.text: He said, "I will ask forgiveness for you from my Lord. Indeed, it is He who is the Forgiving, the Merciful."

98.author: Sarwar
98.text: He said, "I shall ask my Lord to forgive you; He is All-forgiving and All-merciful."

98.author: Shakir
98.text: He said: I will ask for you forgiveness from my Lord; surely He is the Forgiving, the Merciful.

98.author: Wahiduddin Khan
98.text: He said, "I shall certainly ask my Lord to forgive you. Surely, He is the Most Forgiving and Merciful."

98.author: Yusuf Ali
98.text: He said: "Soon will I ask my Lord for forgiveness for you: for he is indeed Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful."

98.author: Transliteration
98.text: Qala sawfa astaghfiru lakum rabbee innahu huwa alghafooru alrraheemu

99.author: Ahmed Ali
99.text: When they went back to Joseph he gave his father and mother a place of honour, and said: "Enter Egypt in peace by the will of God."

99.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
99.text: So when they all reached in Yusuf’s company, he kept his parents close to him, and said, “Enter Egypt, if Allah wills, in safety.”

99.author: Arberry
99.text: So, when they entered unto Joseph, he took his father and mother into his arms saying, 'Enter you into Egypt, if God will, in security.'

99.author: Daryabadi
99.text: Then when they entered unto Yusuf, he betook his parents unto himself. and said: enter Misr, if Allah will, in security.

99.author: Hilali & Khan
99.text: Then, when they entered unto Yusuf (Joseph), he betook his parents to himself and said: "Enter Egypt, if Allah wills, in security."

99.author: Itani
99.text: Then, when they entered into the presence of Joseph, he embraced his parents, and said, “Enter Egypt, God willing, safe and secure.”

99.author: Maududi
99.text: And when they went to Joseph, he took his parents aside and said (to the members of his family): "Enter the city now, and if Allah wills, you shall be secure."

99.author: Mubarakpuri
99.text: Then, when they came in before Yusuf, he took his parents to himself and said: "Enter Egypt, if Allah wills, in security."

99.author: Pickthall
99.text: And when they came in before Joseph, he took his parents unto him, and said: Come into Egypt safe, if Allah will!

99.author: Qarai
99.text: When they entered into the presence of Joseph, he set his parents close to himself, and said, ‘Welcome to Egypt, in safety, God willing!’

99.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
99.text: And when they entered before Joseph, he took his father and mother into his arms and said: 'Welcome to Egypt, safe, if Allah wills'

99.author: Saheeh International
99.text: And when they entered upon Joseph, he took his parents to himself and said, "Enter Egypt, Allah willing, safe [and secure]."

99.author: Sarwar
99.text: When they all came to Joseph, he welcomed his parents and said, "Enter the town in peace, if God wants it to be so."

99.author: Shakir
99.text: Then when they came in to Yusuf, he took his parents to lodge with him and said: Enter safe into Egypt, if Allah please.

99.author: Wahiduddin Khan
99.text: Then, when they presented themselves before Joseph, he drew his parents to him and said, "Welcome to Egypt, in safety, if God wills!"

99.author: Yusuf Ali
99.text: Then when they entered the presence of Joseph, he provided a home for his parents with himself, and said: "Enter ye Egypt (all) in safety if it please Allah."

99.author: Transliteration
99.text: Falamma dakhaloo AAala yoosufa awa ilayhi abawayhi waqala odkhuloo misra in shaa Allahu amineena

100.author: Ahmed Ali
100.text: He seated his parents by his side on the throne; and they fell down before him in homage. "O my father," said Joseph, "this is the meaning of my earlier dream. My Lord has made it come true. He was gracious in getting me out of the prison, and bringing you out of the desert to me after the discord created by Satan between me and my brothers, for my Lord is gracious to whomsoever He please. He is indeed all-knowing and all-wise.

100.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
100.text: And he seated his parents on the throne, and they all prostrated before him; and Yusuf said, “O my father! This is the interpretation of my former dream; my Lord has made it true; and indeed He has bestowed favour upon me, when He brought me out of prison and brought you all from the village, after Satan had created a resentment between me and my brothers; indeed my Lord may make easy whatever He wills; undoubtedly He is the All Knowing, the Wise.”

100.author: Arberry
100.text: And he lifted his father and mother upon the throne; and the others fell down prostrate before him. 'See, father,' he said, 'this is the interpretation of my vision of long ago; my Lord has made it true. He was good to me when He brought me forth from the prison, and again when He brought you out of the desert, after that Satan set at variance me and my brethren. My Lord is gentle to what He will; He is the All-knowing, the All-wise.

100.author: Daryabadi
100.text: And he raised his parents to the throne, and they fell down before him prostrate, And he said: O my father! this is the interpretation of my vision aforetime; My Lord hath now made it come true; and surely He did well by me when he took me forth from the prison, and hath brought you from the desert after the Satan had stirred strife between me and my brethren; verily my Lord is subtilel Unto whomsoever He will. Verily He! only He, is the Knowing, the Wise.

100.author: Hilali & Khan
100.text: And he raised his parents to the throne and they fell down before him prostrate. And he said: "O my father! This is the interpretation of my dream aforetime! My Lord has made it come true! He was indeed good to me, when He took me out of the prison, and brought you (all here) out of the bedouin-life, after Shaitan (Satan) had sown enmity between me and my brothers. Certainly, my Lord is the Most Courteous and Kind unto whom He will. Truly He! Only He is the All-Knowing, the All-Wise.

100.author: Itani
100.text: And he elevated his parents on the throne, and they fell prostrate before him. He said, “Father, this is the fulfillment of my vision of long ago. My Lord has made it come true. He has blessed me, when he released me from prison, and brought you out of the wilderness, after the devil had sown conflict between me and my brothers. My Lord is Most Kind towards whomever He wills. He is the All-knowing, the Most Wise.”

100.author: Maududi
100.text: And after they had entered the city, Joseph raised his parents to the throne beside himself, and they (involuntarily) bowed in prostration before him. Joseph said: "Father! This is the fulfilment of the vision I had before - one that My Lord has caused to come true. He was kind to me when He rescued me from the prison, and brought you from the desert after Satan had stirred discord between me and my brothers. Certainly my Lord is Subtle in the fulfilment of His will; He is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

100.author: Mubarakpuri
100.text: And he raised his parents to the `Arsh and they fell down before him prostrate. And he said: "O my father! This is the interpretation of my dream aforetime! My Lord has made it come true! He was indeed good to me, when He took me out of the prison, and brought you (all here) out of the bedouin life, after Shaytan had sown enmity between me and my brothers. Certainly, my Lord is the Most Courteous and Kind unto whom He wills. Truly, He! Only He is the All-Knowing, the All-Wise.

100.author: Pickthall
100.text: And he placed his parents on the dais and they fell down before him prostrate, and he said: O my father! This is the interpretation of my dream of old. My Lord hath made it true, and He hath shown me kindness, since He took me out of the prison and hath brought you from the desert after Satan had made strife between me and my brethren. Lo! my Lord is tender unto whom He will. He is the Knower, the Wise.

100.author: Qarai
100.text: And he seated his parents high upon the throne, and they fell down prostrate before him. He said, ‘Father! This is the fulfillment of my dream of long ago, which my Lord has made come true. He was certainly gracious to me when He brought me out of the prison and brought you over from the desert after that Satan had incited ill feeling between me and my brothers. Indeed my Lord is all-attentive in bringing about what He wishes. Indeed He is the All-knowing, the All-wise.’

100.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
100.text: He lifted his parents to the throne, and (the others) bowed before him. Joseph said to his father: 'This is the meaning of my vision of long ago, my Lord has verified it. He has been gracious to me. He brought me out of prison and brought you out of the desert after satan had corrupted (the relationship) between me and my brothers. My Lord is Gentle to whom He will. He alone is the Knower, the Wise.

100.author: Saheeh International
100.text: And he raised his parents upon the throne, and they bowed to him in prostration. And he said, "O my father, this is the explanation of my vision of before. My Lord has made it reality. And He was certainly good to me when He took me out of prison and brought you [here] from bedouin life after Satan had induced [estrangement] between me and my brothers. Indeed, my Lord is Subtle in what He wills. Indeed, it is He who is the Knowing, the Wise.

100.author: Sarwar
100.text: He raised his parents on the throne and they prostrated themselves before him (Joseph). He said, "This is the meaning of my dream which God has made come true. He has granted me many favors. He set me free from prison and brought you to me from the desert after having ended the enmity which satan sowed between my brothers and I. My Lord is certainly kind to whomever He wants. It is He who is All-forgiving and All-wise.

100.author: Shakir
100.text: And he raised his parents upon the throne and they fell down in prostration before him, and he said: O my father! this is the significance of my vision of old; my Lord has indeed made it to be true; and He was indeed kind to me when He brought me forth from the prison and brought you from the desert after the Shaitan had sown dissensions between me and my brothers, surely my Lord is benignant to whom He pleases; surely He is the Knowing, the Wise.

100.author: Wahiduddin Khan
100.text: He helped his parents to a couch and they all fell down on their knees before him. He said, "My father, this is the interpretation of my dream. My Lord has made it come true! He was kind to me when He let me out of prison and brought you from the desert after Satan had brought about discord between me and my brethren. My Lord is the best planner in achieving what He will; He is All Knowing, and Truly Wise."

100.author: Yusuf Ali
100.text: And he raised his parents high on the throne (of dignity), and they fell down in prostration, (all) before him. He said: "O my father! this is the fulfilment of my vision of old! Allah hath made it come true! He was indeed good to me when He took me out of prison and brought you (all here) out of the desert, (even) after Satan had sown enmity between me and my brothers. Verily my Lord understandeth best the mysteries of all that He planneth to do, for verily He is full of knowledge and wisdom.

100.author: Transliteration
100.text: WarafaAAa abawayhi AAala alAAarshi wakharroo lahu sujjadan waqala ya abati hatha taweelu ruyaya min qablu qad jaAAalaha rabbee haqqan waqad ahsana bee ith akhrajanee mina alssijni wajaa bikum mina albadwi min baAAdi an nazagha alshshaytanu baynee wabayna ikhwatee inna rabbee lateefun lima yashao innahu huwa alAAaleemu alhakeemu

101.author: Ahmed Ali
101.text: O my Lord, you have given me dominion and taught me the interpretation of dreams; O Creator of the heavens and the earth, You alone are my saviour in this world and the world to come; let me die submitting to You, and place me among the upright."

101.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
101.text: “O my Lord! you have given me a kingdom* and have taught me how to interpret some events; O Creator of the heavens and the earth – you are my Supporter in the world and in the Hereafter; cause me to die as a Muslim, and unite me with those who deserve Your proximity.” (* Prophethood and the rule over Egypt. Prophet Yusuf said this prayer while his death approached him.)

101.author: Arberry
101.text: O my Lord, Thou hast given me to rule, and Thou hast taught me the interpretation of tales. O Thou, the Originator of the heavens and earth, Thou art my Protector in this world and the next. O receive me to Thee in true submission, and join me with the righteous.'

101.author: Daryabadi
101.text: O My Lord! Thou hast given me of the dominion, and hast taught me of the interpretation of discourses, Creator of the heavens and the earth! Thou art my Patron in the world and the Hereafter. Make me to die a Muslim, and join me with the righteous.

101.author: Hilali & Khan
101.text: "My Lord! You have indeed bestowed on me of the sovereignty, and taught me the interpretation of dreams; The (only) Creator of the heavens and the earth! You are my Wali (Protector, Helper, Supporter, Guardian, etc.) in this world and in the Hereafter, cause me to die as a Muslim (the one submitting to Your Will), and join me with the righteous."

101.author: Itani
101.text: “My Lord, You have given me some authority, and taught me some interpretation of events. Initiator of the heavens and the earth; You are my Protector in this life and in the Hereafter. Receive my soul in submission, and unite me with the righteous.”

101.author: Maududi
101.text: My Lord! You have bestowed dominion upon me and have taught me to comprehend the depths of things. O Creator of heavens and earth! You are my Guardian in this world and in the Hereafter. Cause me to die in submission to You, and join me, in the end, with the righteous."

101.author: Mubarakpuri
101.text: "My Lord! You have indeed bestowed on me of the sovereignty, and taught me something of the interpretation of dreams - the (Only) Creator of the heavens and the earth! You are my Wali in this world and in the Hereafter. Cause me to die as a Muslim, and join me with the righteous."

101.author: Pickthall
101.text: O my Lord! Thou hast given me (something) of sovereignty and hast taught me (something) of the interpretation of events - Creator of the heavens and the earth! Thou art my Protecting Guardian in the world and the Hereafter. Make me to die muslim (unto Thee), and join me to the righteous.

101.author: Qarai
101.text: ‘My Lord! You have granted me a share in the kingdom, and taught me the interpretation of dreams. Originator of the heavens and earth! You are my guardian in this world and the Hereafter! Let my death be in submission [to You], and unite me with the Righteous.’

101.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
101.text: Lord, You have given me the kingdom and taught me to interpret visions. Originator of the heavens and the earth, my Guardian in this world and in the Everlasting Life. Let me die in submission (as a Muslim) and let me join the righteous'

101.author: Saheeh International
101.text: My Lord, You have given me [something] of sovereignty and taught me of the interpretation of dreams. Creator of the heavens and earth, You are my protector in this world and in the Hereafter. Cause me to die a Muslim and join me with the righteous."

101.author: Sarwar
101.text: "My Lord, You have given me the kingdom and taught me the meaning of dreams. You are the Creator of the heavens and the earth. You are my Guardian in this world and in the life to come. Make me die as one who has submitted to the Will of God and unite me with the righteous ones."

101.author: Shakir
101.text: My Lord! Thou hast given me of the kingdom and taught me of the interpretation of sayings: Originator of the heavens and the earth! Thou art my guardian in this world and the hereafter; make me die a muslim and join me with the good.

101.author: Wahiduddin Khan
101.text: Then Joseph prayed, "My Lord, You have given me power and taught me the interpretation of dreams. Creator of the heavens and the earth, You are my patron in this world and the Hereafter! Make me die in submission to You and admit me among the righteous."

101.author: Yusuf Ali
101.text: "O my Lord! Thou hast indeed bestowed on me some power, and taught me something of the interpretation of dreams and events,- O Thou Creator of the heavens and the earth! Thou art my Protector in this world and in the Hereafter. Take Thou my soul (at death) as one submitting to Thy will (as a Muslim), and unite me with the righteous."

101.author: Transliteration
101.text: Rabbi qad ataytanee mina almulki waAAallamtanee min taweeli alahadeethi fatira alssamawati waalardi anta waliyyee fee alddunya waalakhirati tawaffanee musliman waalhiqnee bialssaliheena

102.author: Ahmed Ali
102.text: This is news of the unknown We reveal to you, for you were not present when Joseph's brothers agreed on their course of action, and devised their plot.

102.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
102.text: These are some tidings of the Hidden which We divinely reveal to you (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him); and you were not with them when they set their task and when they were scheming.

102.author: Arberry
102.text: That is of the tidings of the Unseen that We reveal to thee; thou wast not with them when they agreed upon their plan, devising.

102.author: Daryabadi
102.text: This is of the tidings of the unseen, which We reveal unto thee; nor wast thou with them when they resolved on their affair while they were plotting.

102.author: Hilali & Khan
102.text: This is of the news of the Ghaib (unseen) which We reveal by Inspiration to you (O Muhammad SAW). You were not (present) with them when they arranged their plan together, and (also, while) they were plotting.

102.author: Itani
102.text: This is news from the past that We reveal to you. You were not present with them when they plotted and agreed on a plan.

102.author: Maududi
102.text: (O Muhammad), this is part of news from the Unseen that We reveal to you for you were not present with them when Joseph's brothers jointly resolved on a plot.

102.author: Mubarakpuri
102.text: That is of the news of the Ghayb (Unseen) which We reveal to you. You were not (present) with them when they arranged their plan together, and (while) they were plotting.

102.author: Pickthall
102.text: This is of the tidings of the Unseen which We inspire in thee (Muhammad). Thou wast not present with them when they fixed their plan and they were scheming.

102.author: Qarai
102.text: These are accounts from the Unseen, which We reveal to you, and you were not with them when they conspired together and schemed.

102.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
102.text: That is of the news We reveal to you of the unseen. You were not present when they agreed upon their plan, scheming.

102.author: Saheeh International
102.text: That is from the news of the unseen which We reveal, [O Muhammad], to you. And you were not with them when they put together their plan while they conspired.

102.author: Sarwar
102.text: This is some of the news of the unseen which We reveal to you, (Muhammad). You were not with them when Joseph's brothers agreed on devising their evil plans.

102.author: Shakir
102.text: This is of the announcements relating to the unseen (which) We reveal to you, and you were not with them when they resolved upon their affair, and they were devising plans.

102.author: Wahiduddin Khan
102.text: These are tidings of the unseen that We reveal to you, [O Prophet], though you were not present with them when they plotted and agreed upon a plan.

102.author: Yusuf Ali
102.text: Such is one of the stories of what happened unseen, which We reveal by inspiration unto thee; nor wast thou (present) with them then when they concerted their plans together in the process of weaving their plots.

102.author: Transliteration
102.text: Thalika min anbai alghaybi nooheehi ilayka wama kunta ladayhim ith ajmaAAoo amrahum wahum yamkuroona

103.author: Ahmed Ali
103.text: Many men will not believe howsoever you wish,

103.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
103.text: And however much you long for, most men will not accept faith.

103.author: Arberry
103.text: Yet, be thou ever so eager, the most part of men believe not.

103.author: Daryabadi
103.text: And most of the people, though thou desiredest ardently, are not going to be believers.

103.author: Hilali & Khan
103.text: And most of mankind will not believe even if you desire it eagerly.

103.author: Itani
103.text: But most people, for all your eagerness, are not believers.

103.author: Maududi
103.text: And most of the people, howsoever you might so desire, are not going to believe.

103.author: Mubarakpuri
103.text: And most of mankind will not believe even if you desire it eagerly.

103.author: Pickthall
103.text: And though thou try much, most men will not believe.

103.author: Qarai
103.text: Yet most people will not have faith, however eager you should be.

103.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
103.text: Even though you are so eager, most people will not believe.

103.author: Saheeh International
103.text: And most of the people, although you strive [for it], are not believers.

103.author: Sarwar
103.text: However hard you try, most people will not believe.

103.author: Shakir
103.text: And most men will not believe though you desire it eagerly.

103.author: Wahiduddin Khan
103.text: Yet most men will not become believers, no matter how eager you may be.

103.author: Yusuf Ali
103.text: Yet no faith will the greater part of mankind have, however ardently thou dost desire it.

103.author: Transliteration
103.text: Wama aktharu alnnasi walaw harasta bimumineena

104.author: Ahmed Ali
104.text: Even though you ask no recompense (for it). It is only a warning for all mankind.

104.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
104.text: You do not ask them any fee in return for it; this is not but an advice to the entire world.

104.author: Arberry
104.text: Thou askest of them no wage for it; it is nothing but a reminder unto all beings.

104.author: Daryabadi
104.text: And thou askest them not any hire therefor; it is but an admonition unto the worlds.

104.author: Hilali & Khan
104.text: And no reward you (O Muhammad SAW) ask of them (those who deny your Prophethood) for it, it (the Quran) is no less than a Reminder and an advice unto the 'Alamin (men and jinns).

104.author: Itani
104.text: You ask them no wage for it. It is only a reminder for all mankind.

104.author: Maududi
104.text: You do not seek from them any recompense for your service. This is merely an admonition to all mankind.

104.author: Mubarakpuri
104.text: And no reward you ask of them for it; it (the Qur'an) is no less than a Reminder unto the `Alamin (men and Jinn).

104.author: Pickthall
104.text: Thou askest them no fee for it. It is naught else than a reminder unto the peoples.

104.author: Qarai
104.text: You do not ask them any reward for it: it is just a reminder for all the nations.

104.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
104.text: You shall ask no wage for it. It is nothing except a reminder to all mankind.

104.author: Saheeh International
104.text: And you do not ask of them for it any payment. It is not except a reminder to the worlds.

104.author: Sarwar
104.text: You do not ask any reward for your preaching (of Our guidance to them). This (Quran) is a guide for the people of the world (human beings and jinn).

104.author: Shakir
104.text: And you do not ask them for a reward for this; it is nothing but a reminder for all mankind.

104.author: Wahiduddin Khan
104.text: You shall not ask them for any reward for this. It is but a reminder for all mankind.

104.author: Yusuf Ali
104.text: And no reward dost thou ask of them for this: it is no less than a message for all creatures.

104.author: Transliteration
104.text: Wama tasaluhum AAalayhi min ajrin in huwa illa thikrun lilAAalameena

105.author: Ahmed Ali
105.text: How many a sign there is in the heavens and the earth which most men pass by and ignore,

105.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
105.text: And how many signs exist in the heavens and the earth, over which most people pass and remain unaware of them!

105.author: Arberry
105.text: How many a sign there is in the heavens and in the earth that they pass by, turning away from it!

105.author: Daryabadi
105.text: And how many a sign in the heavens and the earth they pass by, while they are averters therefrom.

105.author: Hilali & Khan
105.text: And how many a sign in the heavens and the earth they pass by, while they are averse therefrom.

105.author: Itani
105.text: How many a sign in the heavens and the earth do they pass by, paying no attention to them?

105.author: Maududi
105.text: How many are the signs in the heavens and the earth which people pass by without giving any heed!

105.author: Mubarakpuri
105.text: And how many a sign in the heavens and the earth they pass by, while they are averse therefrom.

105.author: Pickthall
105.text: How many a portent is there in the heavens and the earth which they pass by with face averted!

105.author: Qarai
105.text: How many a sign there is in the heavens and the earth that they pass by while they are disregardful of it!

105.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
105.text: How many signs in the heavens and earth do they pass by and turn away from.

105.author: Saheeh International
105.text: And how many a sign within the heavens and earth do they pass over while they, therefrom, are turning away.

105.author: Sarwar
105.text: There is much evidence (of the existence of God) in the heavens and the earth which they see, but ignore.

105.author: Shakir
105.text: And how many a sign in the heavens and the earth which they pass by, yet they turn aside from it.

105.author: Wahiduddin Khan
105.text: And there are many signs in the heavens and the earth that they pass by and give no heed to --

105.author: Yusuf Ali
105.text: And how many Signs in the heavens and the earth do they pass by? Yet they turn (their faces) away from them!

105.author: Transliteration
105.text: Wakaayyin min ayatin fee alssamawati waalardi yamurroona AAalayha wahum AAanha muAAridoona

106.author: Ahmed Ali
106.text: Not only do they not believe in God, but also associate others with Him.

106.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
106.text: And most of them are such that they do not believe in Allah except while ascribing partners (to Him)!

106.author: Arberry
106.text: And the most part of them believe not in God, but they associate other gods with Him.

106.author: Daryabadi
106.text: And most of them believe not in Allah except as they are associators.

106.author: Hilali & Khan
106.text: And most of them believe not in Allah except that they attribute partners unto Him [i.e. they are Mushrikun -polytheists - see Verse 6:121].

106.author: Itani
106.text: And most of them do not believe in God unless they associate others.

106.author: Maududi
106.text: And most of them believe in Allah only when they associate others with Him in His Divinity.

106.author: Mubarakpuri
106.text: And most of them believe not in Allah except that they attribute partners unto Him.

106.author: Pickthall
106.text: And most of them believe not in Allah except that they attribute partners (unto Him).

106.author: Qarai
106.text: And most of them do not believe in Allah without ascribing partners to Him.

106.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
106.text: And most of them do not believe in Allah, but they associate others with Him.

106.author: Saheeh International
106.text: And most of them believe not in Allah except while they associate others with Him.

106.author: Sarwar
106.text: Most of them do not believe in God; they are but pagans.

106.author: Shakir
106.text: And most of them do not believe in Allah without associating others (with Him).

106.author: Wahiduddin Khan
106.text: and most of them, even when they profess belief in God, attribute partners to Him.

106.author: Yusuf Ali
106.text: And most of them believe not in Allah without associating (other as partners) with Him!

106.author: Transliteration
106.text: Wama yuminu aktharuhum biAllahi illa wahum mushrikoona

107.author: Ahmed Ali
107.text: Do they really believe that an all-embracing punishment of God will not come upon them, or the Day of Doom overtake them suddenly while they remain unaware?

107.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
107.text: Do they remain complacent over Allah’s punishment coming and surrounding them, or the Last Day coming suddenly upon them whilst they are unaware?

107.author: Arberry
107.text: Do they feel secure that there shall come upon them no enveloping of the chastisement of God, or that the Hour shall not come upon them suddenly when they are unaware?

107.author: Daryabadi
107.text: Are they secure then against this, that there may come upon them an overwhelming of Allah's torment, or that there may come upon them the Hour on a sudden while they percieve not?

107.author: Hilali & Khan
107.text: Do they then feel secure from the coming against them of the covering veil of the Torment of Allah, or of the coming against them of the (Final) Hour, all of a sudden while they perceive not?

107.author: Itani
107.text: Do they feel secure that a covering of God’s punishment will not come upon them, or that the Hour will not come upon them suddenly, while they are unaware?

107.author: Maududi
107.text: Do they, then, feel secure that an overwhelming chastisement shall not visit them, and the Hour shall not suddenly come upon them without their even perceiving it?

107.author: Mubarakpuri
107.text: Do they then feel secure from the coming against them of the covering veil of the torment of Allah, or of the coming against them of the (Final) Hour, all of a sudden while they perceive not

107.author: Pickthall
107.text: Deem they themselves secure from the coming on them of a pall of Allah's punishment, or the coming of the Hour suddenly while they are unaware?

107.author: Qarai
107.text: Do they feel secure from being overtaken by a blanket punishment from Allah, or being overtaken by the Hour, suddenly, while they are unaware?

107.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
107.text: Do they feel secure that the punishment of Allah will envelop them, or that the Hour will overtake them suddenly when they are unaware?

107.author: Saheeh International
107.text: Then do they feel secure that there will not come to them an overwhelming [aspect] of the punishment of Allah or that the Hour will not come upon them suddenly while they do not perceive?

107.author: Sarwar
107.text: Do they feel safe from God's overwhelming torment or of the sudden approach of the Day of Judgment while they are unaware?

107.author: Shakir
107.text: Do they then feel secure that there may come to them an extensive chastisement from Allah or (that) the hour may come to them suddenly while they do not perceive?

107.author: Wahiduddin Khan
107.text: Do they feel secure that the all-encompassing punishment of God will not come upon them, or that the Last Hour will not come upon them suddenly when they least expect it?

107.author: Yusuf Ali
107.text: Do they then feel secure from the coming against them of the covering veil of the wrath of Allah,- or of the coming against them of the (final) Hour all of a sudden while they perceive not?

107.author: Transliteration
107.text: Afaaminoo an tatiyahum ghashiyatun min AAathabi Allahi aw tatiyahumu alssaAAatu baghtatan wahum la yashAAuroona

108.author: Ahmed Ali
108.text: Say: "My way, and that of my followers, is to call you to God with full perception. All glory to God, I am not an idolater."

108.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
108.text: Proclaim, “This is my path – I call towards Allah; I, and whoever follows me, are upon perception; and Purity is to Allah – and I am not of the polytheists.”

108.author: Arberry
108.text: Say: 'This is my way. I call to God with sure knowledge, I and whoever follows after me. To God be glory! And I am not among the idolaters.'

108.author: Daryabadi
108.text: Say thou: this is my way: I call unto Allah resting upon an insight---I, and whosoever followeth me. Hallowed be Allah: and I am not of the associators.

108.author: Hilali & Khan
108.text: Say (O Muhammad SAW): "This is my way; I invite unto Allah (i.e. to the Oneness of Allah - Islamic Monotheism) with sure knowledge, I and whosoever follows me (also must invite others to Allah i.e. to the Oneness of Allah - Islamic Monotheism) with sure knowledge. And Glorified and Exalted be Allah (above all that they associate as partners with Him). And I am not of the Mushrikun (polytheists, pagans, idolaters and disbelievers in the Oneness of Allah; those who worship others along with Allah or set up rivals or partners to Allah)."

108.author: Itani
108.text: Say, “This is my way; I invite to God, based on clear knowledge—I and whoever follows me. Glory be to God; and I am not of the polytheists.”

108.author: Maududi
108.text: Tell them plainly: "This is my way: I call you to Allah, on the basis of clear perception - both I and those who follow me. Allah - Glory be to Him - is free of every imperfection. I have nothing to do with those who associate others with Allah in His Divinity."

108.author: Mubarakpuri
108.text: Say "This is my way; I invite unto Allah with sure knowledge, I and whosoever follows me. And Glorified and Exalted be Allah. And I am not of the idolators."

108.author: Pickthall
108.text: Say: This is my Way: I call on Allah with sure knowledge. I and whosoever followeth me - Glory be to Allah! - and I am not of the idolaters.

108.author: Qarai
108.text: Say, ‘This is my way. I summon to Allah with insight—I and he who follows me. Immaculate is Allah, and I am not one of the polytheists.’

108.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
108.text: Say: 'This is my Path. I call to Allah with sure knowledge, I and my followers. Exaltations be to Allah! I am not among the idolaters'

108.author: Saheeh International
108.text: Say, "This is my way; I invite to Allah with insight, I and those who follow me. And exalted is Allah; and I am not of those who associate others with Him."

108.author: Sarwar
108.text: (Muhammad), say, "This is my way. I and all my followers invite you to God with proper understanding. God is most Glorious. I am not a pagan."

108.author: Shakir
108.text: Say: This is my way: I call to Allah, I and those who follow me being certain, and glory be to Allah, and I am not one of the polytheists.

108.author: Wahiduddin Khan
108.text: Say, "This is my way; on the basis of sure knowledge, I call on you to have faith in God, I and those who follow me. God is Holy; I am not one of those who associate partners with God."

108.author: Yusuf Ali
108.text: Say thou: "This is my way: I do invite unto Allah,- on evidence clear as the seeing with one's eyes,- I and whoever follows me. Glory to Allah! and never will I join gods with Allah!"

108.author: Transliteration
108.text: Qul hathihi sabeelee adAAoo ila Allahi AAala baseeratin ana wamani ittabaAAanee wasubhana Allahi wama ana mina almushrikeena

109.author: Ahmed Ali
109.text: All the apostles We had sent before you were men of those regions, to whom We sent Our revelations. Have they not travelled on the earth and seen what befell the people before them? Surely the abode of the Hereafter is better for those who fear straying from the right path. Do you not understand?

109.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
109.text: And all the Noble Messengers We sent before you, were exclusively men – towards whom We sent the divine revelations, and were dwellers of townships; so have not these people travelled in the land and seen what sort of fate befell those before them? And undoubtedly the abode of the Hereafter is better for the pious; so do you not have sense?

109.author: Arberry
109.text: We sent not forth any before thee, but men We revealed to of the people living in the cities. Have they not journeyed in the land? Have they not beheld how was the end of those before them?. Surely the abode of the world to come is better for those that are godfearing. What, do you not understand?

109.author: Daryabadi
109.text: And We sent not before thee any save men unto whom We revealed from among the people of the towns. Have then they not travelled about in the land that they might observe of what wise hath been the end of those before them! And surely the abode of the, Hereafter is best for those who fear! Reflect then ye not!

109.author: Hilali & Khan
109.text: And We sent not before you (as Messengers) any but men, whom We inspired from among the people of townships. Have they not travelled through the earth and seen what was the end of those who were before them? And verily, the home of the Hereafter is the best for those who fear Allah and obey Him (by abstaining from sins and evil deeds, and by performing righteous good deeds). Do you not then understand?

109.author: Itani
109.text: We did not send before you except men, whom We inspired, from the people of the towns. Have they not roamed the earth and seen the consequences for those before them? The Home of the Hereafter is better for those who are righteous. Do you not understand?

109.author: Maududi
109.text: The Messengers whom We raised before you, (O Muhammad), and to whom We sent down revelations, were only human beings, and were from among those living in earthly habitations. Have these people not travelled in the earth that they may observe the end of their predecessors? Certainly the abode of the Hereafter is much better for those (who accepted the call of the Messengers and) acted in a God-fearing manner. Will you still not act with good sense?

109.author: Mubarakpuri
109.text: And We sent not before you (as Messengers) any but men unto whom We revealed, from among the people of townships. Have they not traveled in the land and seen what was the end of those who were before them And verily, the home of the Hereafter is the best for those who have Taqwa. Do you not then understand

109.author: Pickthall
109.text: We sent not before thee (any messengers) save men whom We inspired from among the folk of the townships - Have they not travelled in the land and seen the nature of the consequence for those who were before them? And verily the abode of the Hereafter, for those who ward off (evil), is best. Have ye then no sense? -

109.author: Qarai
109.text: We did not send [any apostles] before you except as men from among the people of the towns, to whom We revealed. Have they not travelled over the land so that they may observe how was the fate of those who were before them? And the abode of the Hereafter is surely better for those who are Godwary. Do you not exercise your reason?

109.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
109.text: We did not send any before you from the inhabitants of the village, but men to whom a revelation was sent. Have they not traveled in the land and seen what was the end of those before them? Better is the dwelling in the Everlasting Life for those who keep from evil. Do you not understand?

109.author: Saheeh International
109.text: And We sent not before you [as messengers] except men to whom We revealed from among the people of cities. So have they not traveled through the earth and observed how was the end of those before them? And the home of the Hereafter is best for those who fear Allah; then will you not reason?

109.author: Sarwar
109.text: The Messengers whom We sent before you were mere men of the people of the towns. We gave them revelations. Have they (the unbelievers) not travelled sufficiently through the land to see how terrible the end was of those who lived before. The next life is, certainly, better for the pious ones. Will you not then take heed?

109.author: Shakir
109.text: And We have not sent before you but men from (among) the people of the towns, to whom We sent revelations. Have they not then travelled in the land and seen what was the end of those before them? And certainly the abode of the hereafter is best for those who guard (against evil); do you not then understand?

109.author: Wahiduddin Khan
109.text: All the messengers We sent before you [Muhammad] were human beings to whom We made revelations; they were men chosen from the people of their towns. Did they not travel across the earth and see the end of those before them? Those who are mindful of God prefer the life to come. Will you not then understand?

109.author: Yusuf Ali
109.text: Nor did We send before thee (as messengers) any but men, whom we did inspire,- (men) living in human habitations. Do they not travel through the earth, and see what was the end of those before them? But the home of the hereafter is best, for those who do right. Will ye not then understand?

109.author: Transliteration
109.text: Wama arsalna min qablika illa rijalan noohee ilayhim min ahli alqura afalam yaseeroo fee alardi fayanthuroo kayfa kana AAaqibatu allatheena min qablihim waladaru alakhirati khayrun lillatheena ittaqaw afala taAAqiloona

110.author: Ahmed Ali
110.text: When the apostles despaired and thought they were made false promises, Our help arrived, and We delivered whom We pleased; but never will Our punishment be averted from the sinners.

110.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
110.text: To the extent that when the Noble Messengers lost hope from the visible means, and the people thought that they had spoken wrongly, Our help came to them – therefore whoever We willed was saved; and Our punishment is never averted from the guilty.

110.author: Arberry
110.text: Till, when the Messengers despaired, deeming they were counted liars, Our help came to them and whosoever We willed was delivered. Our might will never be turned back from the people of the sinners.

110.author: Daryabadi
110.text: Respited were they until when the apostles had despaired and imagined that they were deluded, there came unto them Our succour; then whomsoever We willed was delivered. las And Our wrath is not warded off from a people sinful.

110.author: Hilali & Khan
110.text: (They were reprieved) until, when the Messengers gave up hope and thought that they were denied (by their people), then came to them Our Help, and whomsoever We willed were delivered. And Our Punishment cannot be warded off from the people who are Mujrimun (criminals, disobedients to Allah, sinners, disbelievers, polytheists).

110.author: Itani
110.text: Until, when the messengers have despaired, and thought that they were rejected, Our help came to them. We save whomever We will, and Our severity is not averted from the guilty people.

110.author: Maududi
110.text: (It also happened with the earlier Messengers that for long they preached and people paid no heed) until the Messengers despaired of their people, and the people also fancied that they had been told lies (by the Messengers), then suddenly Our help came to the Messengers. And when such an occasion comes We rescue whom We will; as for the guilty, Our chastisement cannot be averted from them.

110.author: Mubarakpuri
110.text: (They were reprieved) until, when the Messengers gave up hope and thought that they were denied, then came to them Our help, and whomsoever We willed were rescued. And Our punishment cannot be warded off from the people who are criminals.

110.author: Pickthall
110.text: Till, when the messengers despaired and thought that they were denied, then came unto them Our help, and whom We would was saved. And Our wrath cannot be warded from the guilty.

110.author: Qarai
110.text: When the apostles lost hope and they thought that they had been told lies, Our help came to them, and We delivered whomever We wished, and Our punishment will not be averted from the guilty lot.

110.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
110.text: And when at length Our Messengers despaired and reckoned that they would be belied, Our help came down to them, saving whom We pleased. OurMight cannot be withheld from the guilty nation.

110.author: Saheeh International
110.text: [They continued] until, when the messengers despaired and were certain that they had been denied, there came to them Our victory, and whoever We willed was saved. And Our punishment cannot be repelled from the people who are criminals.

110.author: Sarwar
110.text: When at last the Messengers lost all hope of achieving success in their task and thought that everyone had called them liars, We gave them victory and saved whomever We chose to save. The guilty ones can not escape Our wrath.

110.author: Shakir
110.text: Until when the apostles despaired and the people became sure that they were indeed told a lie, Our help came to them and whom We pleased was delivered; and Our punishment is not averted from the guilty people.

110.author: Wahiduddin Khan
110.text: When the messengers lost all hopes and thought that they had been told lies, Our help came to them: We saved whoever We pleased, but Our punishment will not be averted from the guilty.

110.author: Yusuf Ali
110.text: (Respite will be granted) until, when the messengers give up hope (of their people) and (come to) think that they were treated as liars, there reaches them Our help, and those whom We will are delivered into safety. But never will be warded off our punishment from those who are in sin.

110.author: Transliteration
110.text: Hatta itha istayasa alrrusulu wathannoo annahum qad kuthiboo jaahum nasruna fanujjiya man nashao wala yuraddu basuna AAani alqawmi almujrimeena

111.author: Ahmed Ali
111.text: Verily in their accounts is a lesson for men of wisdom. This is not a fictitious tale, but a verification of earlier Books, and a clear exposition of everything, and a guidance and grace for those who believe.

111.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
111.text: Indeed in their tidings is enlightenment for the men of understanding; this (the Qur’an) is not some made up story but a confirmation of the Books before it, and a detailed explanation of all things, and a guidance and a mercy for the Muslims.

111.author: Arberry
111.text: In their stories is surely a lesson to men possessed of minds; it is not a tale forged, but a confirmation of what is before it, and a distinguishing of every thing, and a guidance, and a mercy to a people who believe.

111.author: Daryabadi
111.text: Assuredly in their stories is a lesson for men of understanding. It is not a discourse fabricated, but a confirmation of that which went before it, and a detailing of everything, and a guidance and a mercy unto a people who believe.

111.author: Hilali & Khan
111.text: Indeed in their stories, there is a lesson for men of understanding. It (the Quran) is not a forged statement but a confirmation of the Allah's existing Books [the Taurat (Torah), the Injeel (Gospel) and other Scriptures of Allah] and a detailed explanation of everything and a guide and a Mercy for the people who believe.

111.author: Itani
111.text: In their stories is a lesson for those who possess intelligence. This is not a fabricated tale, but a confirmation of what came before it, and a detailed explanation of all things, and guidance, and mercy for people who believe.

111.author: Maududi
111.text: Certainly in the stories of the bygone people there is a lesson for people of understanding. What is being narrated in the Qur'an is no fabrication; it is rather confirmation of the Books that preceded it, and a detailed exposition of everything, and a guidance and mercy for people of faith.

111.author: Mubarakpuri
111.text: Indeed in their stories, there is a lesson for men of understanding. It (the Qur'an) is not a forged statement but a confirmation of that which was before it and a detailed explanation of everything and a guide and a mercy for the people who believe.

111.author: Pickthall
111.text: In their history verily there is a lesson for men of understanding. It is no invented story but a confirmation of the existing (Scripture) and a detailed explanation of everything, and a guidance and a mercy for folk who believe.

111.author: Qarai
111.text: There is certainly a moral in their accounts for those who possess intellect. This [Quran] is not a fabricated discourse; rather, it is a confirmation of what was [revealed] before it, and an elaboration of all things, and a guidance and mercy for a people who have faith.

111.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
111.text: Indeed, in their stories is a lesson for those of understanding. This is no forged tale, rather, it is a confirmation of the previous, a distinguishing of all things, a guidance and a mercy to a nation who believe.

111.author: Saheeh International
111.text: There was certainly in their stories a lesson for those of understanding. Never was the Qur'an a narration invented, but a confirmation of what was before it and a detailed explanation of all things and guidance and mercy for a people who believe.

111.author: Sarwar
111.text: In their story, there is a lesson for the people of understanding. It is not a legend but a confirmation of what exists (in the Torah). It (the Quran) has details about everything. It is a guide and mercy for those who have faith.

111.author: Shakir
111.text: In their histories there is certainly a lesson for men of understanding. It is not a narrative which could be forged, but a verification of what is before it and a distinct explanation of all things and a guide and a mercy to a people who believe.

111.author: Wahiduddin Khan
111.text: In their stories there is a lesson for men of understanding. This [Quran] is no invented tale, but a confirmation of the previous [scripture] and a detailed explanation of all things as well as guidance and mercy to true believers.

111.author: Yusuf Ali
111.text: There is, in their stories, instruction for men endued with understanding. It is not a tale invented, but a confirmation of what went before it,- a detailed exposition of all things, and a guide and a mercy to any such as believe.

111.author: Transliteration
111.text: Laqad kana fee qasasihim AAibratun liolee alalbabi ma kana hadeethan yuftara walakin tasdeeqa allathee bayna yadayhi watafseela kulli shayin wahudan warahmatan liqawmin yuminoona
